contact.locationName: Toronto City Hall, 10th Floor, West Tower

noticeDescription:
City of Toronto Act, 2006 Public Notice

 

Request for Temporary Delegated Authority to the General Manager, Transportation Services, to Approve Temporary Road Closures Up To, and Including 90 Consecutive Days, for the Purpose of Managing Traffic During Preparation for, During, and after the FIFA World Cup 2026

 

Toronto City Council will be considering recommendations to amend City of Toronto Municipal Code Chapter 27, Council Procedures, in order to remove section references to Chapter 937 within §27-18.4(6)(b) to avoid future amendments to Chapter 27 when delegating authority for temporary road closures under Chapter 937.

 

It is proposed that the amendments be adopted at the City Council Meeting to be held on April 22, 23 and 24, 2026, or at a subsequent meeting.

 

The report from the Executive Director, FIFA World Cup 2026 Toronto Secretariat and General Manager, Transportation Services, titled “FWC26 Toronto Mobility Plan – Temporary Adjustments to Traffic and Parking Regulations”, has the following recommendation:

 

6. City Council amend §27-18.4B(6)(b) of City of Toronto Municipal Code Chapter 27, Council Procedures to omit references to Chapter 937, where authority to close roads has been delegated to the General Manager, Transportation Services.

 

To view or obtain a copy of the report, please visit the City’s website at:

https://secure.toronto.ca/council/#/committees/2468/27178

 

At its meeting to be held in person and by video conference on Wednesday, April 15, 2026 at 9:30 a.m., or as soon as possible thereafter, the Executive Committee of Toronto City Council will hear from any person or by his or her counsel, agent, or solicitor, who wishes to speak to the matter. The meeting details are as follows:

 

Date: April 15, 2026

Time: 9:30 a.m., or as soon as possible thereafter

Location: By Video Conference or Committee Room 1, Toronto City Hall,

100 Queen Street West

 

To submit written comments or make a presentation to the Executive Committee on April 15, 2026, please contact the Committee no later than 12:00 p.m. on April 14, 2026:

 

Executive Committee

City Clerk’s Office

Toronto City Hall

100 Queen Street West, 2nd Floor

Toronto, ON M5H 2N2

Phone: 416-392-7033; Fax: 416-392-2980

Email: exc@toronto.ca

 

For further information concerning the report, please contact:

 

Sharon Bollenbach

Executive Director, FIFA World Cup 2026, Toronto Secretariat

Phone: 416-338-8779

Email: sharon.bollenbach@toronto.ca

 

            or

 

Ashley Curtis

Acting General Manager, Transportation Services

Phone: 416-392-0170

E-mail: Ashley.Curtis@toronto.ca

 

Any comments received after the Committee meeting will be forwarded to City Council.

 

The proposed amendments are subject to the decisions of the Executive Committee and City Council. Consequently, the proposed amendments may be modified. Additional or other amendments to the above-noted By-law, including amendments not contemplated or considered in the proposal by the City Clerk, may occur as a result of the decisions of the Committee and City Council.

 

If this matter is postponed at the Committee meeting or Council meeting or considered at a subsequent Committee meeting, no additional notice will be provided other than the information on the subsequent Committee or Council agenda. Please contact the above noted City official if you require notice in these cases.

 

The Executive Committee will make its recommendations on April15, 2026, which will be forwarded for consideration to City Council at its meeting on April 22, 23 and 24, 2026 or at a subsequent meeting

 

Notice to people writing and making presentations to Executive Committee:

The City of Toronto Act, 2006 and the City of Toronto Municipal Code authorize the City of Toronto to collect any personal information in your communication or presentation to City Council or its committees. The City collects this information to enable it to make informed decisions on the relevant issue(s). If you are submitting letters, faxes, e-mails, presentations or other communications to the City, you should be aware that your name and the fact that you communicated with the City will become part of the public record and will appear on the City's website. The City will also make your communication and any personal information in it – such as your postal address, telephone number or e-mail address – available to the public unless you expressly request the City to remove it.

 

Many Committee, Board, and Advisory Body meetings are broadcast live over the internet for the public to view. If you speak at the meeting you will appear in the video broadcast. Video broadcasts are archived and continue to be publicly available.

 

If you want to learn more about why and how the City collects your information, write to the City Clerk's Office, City Hall, 100 Queen Street West, Toronto ON M5H 2N2 or call 416-392-7033.
